speaker: COUNTESS
text: I will now hear. What say you of this

speaker: COUNTESS
text: gentlewoman?

speaker: STEWARD
text: Madam, the care I have had to even your

speaker: STEWARD
text: content I wish might be found in the calendar of

speaker: STEWARD
text: my past endeavors, for then we wound our modesty

speaker: STEWARD
text: and make foul the clearness of our deservings

speaker: STEWARD
text: when of ourselves we publish them.

speaker: COUNTESS
text:
What does this knave here?
                 
                To
                 
                Fool
                .
               Get

speaker: COUNTESS
text: you gone, sirrah. The complaints I have heard of

speaker: COUNTESS
text: you I do not all believe. ’Tis my slowness that I do

speaker: COUNTESS
text: not, for I know you lack not folly to commit them

speaker: COUNTESS
text: and have ability enough to make such knaveries

speaker: COUNTESS
text: yours.

speaker: FOOL
text: ’Tis not unknown to you, madam, I am a poor

speaker: FOOL
text: fellow.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Well, sir.

speaker: FOOL
text: No, madam, ’tis not so well that I am poor,

speaker: FOOL
text: though many of the rich are damned. But if I may

speaker: FOOL
text: have your Ladyship’s good will to go to the world,

speaker: FOOL
text: Isbel the woman and I will do as we may.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Wilt thou needs be a beggar?

speaker: FOOL
text: I do beg your good will in this case.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: In what case?

speaker: FOOL
text: In Isbel’s case and mine own. Service is no heritage,

speaker: FOOL
text: and I think I shall never have the blessing of

speaker: FOOL
text: God till I have issue o’ my body, for they say bairns

speaker: FOOL
text: are blessings.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Tell me thy reason why thou wilt marry.

speaker: FOOL
text: My poor body, madam, requires it. I am driven

speaker: FOOL
text: on by the flesh, and he must needs go that the devil

speaker: FOOL
text: drives.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Is this all your Worship’s reason?

speaker: FOOL
text: Faith, madam, I have other holy reasons, such

speaker: FOOL
text: as they are.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: May the world know them?

speaker: FOOL
text: I have been, madam, a wicked creature, as you

speaker: FOOL
text: and all flesh and blood are, and indeed I do marry

speaker: FOOL
text: that I may repent.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Thy marriage sooner than thy wickedness.

speaker: FOOL
text: I am out o’ friends, madam, and I hope to have

speaker: FOOL
text: friends for my wife’s sake.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Such friends are thine enemies, knave.

speaker: FOOL
text: You’re shallow, madam, in great friends, for the

speaker: FOOL
text: knaves come to do that for me which I am aweary

speaker: FOOL
text: of. He that ears my land spares my team and gives

speaker: FOOL
text: me leave to in the crop; if I be his cuckold, he’s my

speaker: FOOL
text: drudge. He that comforts my wife is the cherisher

speaker: FOOL
text: of my flesh and blood; he that cherishes my flesh

speaker: FOOL
text: and blood loves my flesh and blood; he that loves

speaker: FOOL
text: my flesh and blood is my friend. Ergo, he that

speaker: FOOL
text: kisses my wife is my friend. If men could be contented

speaker: FOOL
text: to be what they are, there were no fear in

speaker: FOOL
text: marriage, for young Charbon the Puritan and old

speaker: FOOL
text: Poysam the Papist, howsome’er their hearts are

speaker: FOOL
text: severed in religion, their heads are both one; they

speaker: FOOL
text: may jowl horns together like any deer i’ th’ herd.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Wilt thou ever be a foul-mouthed and

speaker: COUNTESS
text: calumnious knave?

speaker: FOOL
text: A prophet I, madam, and I speak the truth the

speaker: FOOL
text: next way:

speaker: FOOL
text: For I the ballad will repeat

speaker: FOOL
text: Which men full true shall find:

speaker: FOOL
text: Your marriage comes by destiny;

speaker: FOOL
text: Your cuckoo sings by kind.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Get you gone, sir. I’ll talk with you more

speaker: COUNTESS
text: anon.

speaker: STEWARD
text: May it please you, madam, that he bid Helen

speaker: STEWARD
text: come to you. Of her I am to speak.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Sirrah, tell my gentlewoman I would speak

speaker: COUNTESS
text: with her—Helen, I mean.

speaker: FOOL
text: “Was this fair face the cause,” quoth she,

speaker: FOOL
text: “Why the Grecians sackèd Troy?

speaker: FOOL
text: Fond done, done fond.

speaker: FOOL
text: Was this King Priam’s joy?”

speaker: FOOL
text: With that she sighèd as she stood,

speaker: FOOL
text: With that she sighèd as she stood,

speaker: FOOL
text: And gave this sentence then:

speaker: FOOL
text: “Among nine bad if one be good,

speaker: FOOL
text: Among nine bad if one be good,

speaker: FOOL
text: There’s yet one good in ten.”

speaker: COUNTESS
text: What, one good in ten? You corrupt the

speaker: COUNTESS
text: song, sirrah.

speaker: FOOL
text: One good woman in ten, madam, which is a

speaker: FOOL
text: purifying o’ th’ song. Would God would serve the

speaker: FOOL
text: world so all the year! We’d find no fault with the

speaker: FOOL
text: tithe-woman if I were the parson. One in ten,

speaker: FOOL
text: quoth he? An we might have a good woman born

speaker: FOOL
text: but or every blazing star or at an earthquake,

speaker: FOOL
text: ’twould mend the lottery well. A man may draw his

speaker: FOOL
text: heart out ere he pluck one.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: You’ll be gone, sir knave, and do as I command

speaker: COUNTESS
text: you!

speaker: FOOL
text: That man should be at woman’s command, and

speaker: FOOL
text: yet no hurt done! Though honesty be no Puritan,

speaker: FOOL
text: yet it will do no hurt; it will wear the surplice of

speaker: FOOL
text: humility over the black gown of a big heart. I am

speaker: FOOL
text: going, forsooth. The business is for Helen to come

speaker: FOOL
text: hither.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Well, now.

speaker: STEWARD
text: I know, madam, you love your gentlewoman

speaker: STEWARD
text: entirely.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Faith, I do. Her father bequeathed her to

speaker: COUNTESS
text: me, and she herself, without other advantage, may

speaker: COUNTESS
text: lawfully make title to as much love as she finds.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: There is more owing her than is paid, and more

speaker: COUNTESS
text: shall be paid her than she’ll demand.

speaker: STEWARD
text: Madam, I was very late more near her than I

speaker: STEWARD
text: think she wished me. Alone she was and did communicate

speaker: STEWARD
text: to herself her own words to her own

speaker: STEWARD
text: ears; she thought, I dare vow for her, they touched

speaker: STEWARD
text: not any stranger sense. Her matter was she loved

speaker: STEWARD
text: your son. Fortune, she said, was no goddess, that

speaker: STEWARD
text: had put such difference betwixt their two estates;

speaker: STEWARD
text: Love no god, that would not extend his might only

speaker: STEWARD
text: where qualities were level; Dian no queen of virgins,

speaker: STEWARD
text: that would suffer her poor knight surprised

speaker: STEWARD
text: without rescue in the first assault or ransom afterward.

speaker: STEWARD
text: This she delivered in the most bitter touch

speaker: STEWARD
text: of sorrow that e’er I heard virgin exclaim in, which

speaker: STEWARD
text: I held my duty speedily to acquaint you withal,

speaker: STEWARD
text: sithence in the loss that may happen it concerns

speaker: STEWARD
text: you something to know it.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: You have discharged this honestly. Keep it

speaker: COUNTESS
text: to yourself. Many likelihoods informed me of this

speaker: COUNTESS
text: before, which hung so tott’ring in the balance that

speaker: COUNTESS
text: I could neither believe nor misdoubt. Pray you

speaker: COUNTESS
text: leave me. Stall this in your bosom, and I thank you

speaker: COUNTESS
text: for your honest care. I will speak with you further

speaker: COUNTESS
text: anon.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Even so it was with me when I was young.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: If ever we are nature’s, these are ours. This thorn

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Doth to our rose of youth rightly belong.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Our blood to us, this to our blood is born.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: It is the show and seal of nature’s truth,

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Where love’s strong passion is impressed in youth.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: By our remembrances of days foregone,

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Such were our faults, or then we thought them none.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Her eye is sick on ’t, I observe her now.

speaker: HELEN
text: What is your pleasure, madam?

speaker: COUNTESS
text: You know, Helen, I am a mother to you.

speaker: HELEN
text: Mine honorable mistress.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Nay, a mother.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Why not a mother? When I said “a mother,”

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Methought you saw a serpent. What’s in “mother”

speaker: COUNTESS
text: That you start at it? I say I am your mother

speaker: COUNTESS
text: And put you in the catalogue of those

speaker: COUNTESS
text: That were enwombèd mine. ’Tis often seen

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Adoption strives with nature, and choice breeds

speaker: COUNTESS
text: A native slip to us from foreign seeds.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: You ne’er oppressed me with a mother’s groan,

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Yet I express to you a mother’s care.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: God’s mercy, maiden, does it curd thy blood

speaker: COUNTESS
text: To say I am thy mother? What’s the matter,

speaker: COUNTESS
text: That this distempered messenger of wet,

speaker: COUNTESS
text: The many-colored Iris, rounds thine eye?

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Why? That you are my daughter?

speaker: HELEN
text: That I am not.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: I say I am your mother.

speaker: HELEN
text: Pardon, madam.

speaker: HELEN
text: The Count Rossillion cannot be my brother.

speaker: HELEN
text: I am from humble, he from honored name;

speaker: HELEN
text: No note upon my parents, his all noble.

speaker: HELEN
text: My master, my dear lord he is, and I

speaker: HELEN
text: His servant live and will his vassal die.

speaker: HELEN
text: He must not be my brother.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Nor I your mother?

speaker: HELEN
text: You are my mother, madam. Would you were—

speaker: HELEN
text: So that my lord your son were not my brother—

speaker: HELEN
text: Indeed my mother! Or were you both our mothers,

speaker: HELEN
text: I care no more for than I do for heaven,

speaker: HELEN
text: So I were not his sister. Can ’t no other

speaker: HELEN
text: But, I your daughter, he must be my brother?

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Yes, Helen, you might be my daughter-in-law.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: God shield you mean it not! “Daughter” and “mother”

speaker: COUNTESS
text: So strive upon your pulse. What, pale again?

speaker: COUNTESS
text: My fear hath catched your fondness! Now I see

speaker: COUNTESS
text: The mystery of your loneliness and find

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Your salt tears’ head. Now to all sense ’tis gross:

speaker: COUNTESS
text: You love my son. Invention is ashamed

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Against the proclamation of thy passion

speaker: COUNTESS
text: To say thou dost not. Therefore tell me true,

speaker: COUNTESS
text: But tell me then ’tis so, for, look, thy cheeks

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Confess it th’ one to th’ other, and thine eyes

speaker: COUNTESS
text: See it so grossly shown in thy behaviors

speaker: COUNTESS
text: That in their kind they speak it. Only sin

speaker: COUNTESS
text: And hellish obstinacy tie thy tongue

speaker: COUNTESS
text: That truth should be suspected. Speak. Is ’t so?

speaker: COUNTESS
text: If it be so, you have wound a goodly clew;

speaker: COUNTESS
text: If it be not, forswear ’t; howe’er, I charge thee,

speaker: COUNTESS
text: As heaven shall work in me for thine avail,

speaker: COUNTESS
text: To tell me truly.

speaker: HELEN
text: Good madam, pardon me.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Do you love my son?

speaker: HELEN
text: Your pardon, noble mistress.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Love you my son?

speaker: HELEN
text: Do not you love him, madam?

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Go not about. My love hath in ’t a bond

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Whereof the world takes note. Come, come, disclose

speaker: COUNTESS
text: The state of your affection, for your passions

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Have to the full appeached.

speaker: HELEN
text: Then I confess

speaker: HELEN
text: Here on my knee before high heaven and you

speaker: HELEN
text: That before you and next unto high heaven

speaker: HELEN
text: I love your son.

speaker: HELEN
text: My friends were poor but honest; so ’s my love.

speaker: HELEN
text: Be not offended, for it hurts not him

speaker: HELEN
text: That he is loved of me. I follow him not

speaker: HELEN
text: By any token of presumptuous suit,

speaker: HELEN
text: Nor would I have him till I do deserve him,

speaker: HELEN
text: Yet never know how that desert should be.

speaker: HELEN
text: I know I love in vain, strive against hope,

speaker: HELEN
text: Yet in this captious and intenible sieve

speaker: HELEN
text: I still pour in the waters of my love

speaker: HELEN
text: And lack not to lose still. Thus, Indian-like,

speaker: HELEN
text: Religious in mine error, I adore

speaker: HELEN
text: The sun that looks upon his worshipper

speaker: HELEN
text: But knows of him no more. My dearest madam,

speaker: HELEN
text: Let not your hate encounter with my love

speaker: HELEN
text: For loving where you do; but if yourself,

speaker: HELEN
text: Whose agèd honor cites a virtuous youth,

speaker: HELEN
text: Did ever in so true a flame of liking

speaker: HELEN
text: Wish chastely and love dearly, that your Dian

speaker: HELEN
text: Was both herself and Love, O then give pity

speaker: HELEN
text: To her whose state is such that cannot choose

speaker: HELEN
text: But lend and give where she is sure to lose;

speaker: HELEN
text: That seeks not to find that her search implies,

speaker: HELEN
text: But riddle-like lives sweetly where she dies.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Had you not lately an intent—speak truly—

speaker: COUNTESS
text: To go to Paris?

speaker: HELEN
text: Madam, I had.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Wherefore?

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Tell true.

speaker: HELEN
text: I will tell truth, by grace itself I swear.

speaker: HELEN
text: You know my father left me some prescriptions

speaker: HELEN
text: Of rare and proved effects, such as his reading

speaker: HELEN
text: And manifest experience had collected

speaker: HELEN
text: For general sovereignty; and that he willed me

speaker: HELEN
text: In heedfull’st reservation to bestow them

speaker: HELEN
text: As notes whose faculties inclusive were

speaker: HELEN
text: More than they were in note. Amongst the rest

speaker: HELEN
text: There is a remedy, approved, set down,

speaker: HELEN
text: To cure the desperate languishings whereof

speaker: HELEN
text: The King is rendered lost.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: This was your motive for Paris, was it? Speak.

speaker: HELEN
text: My lord your son made me to think of this;

speaker: HELEN
text: Else Paris, and the medicine, and the King

speaker: HELEN
text: Had from the conversation of my thoughts

speaker: HELEN
text: Haply been absent then.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: But think you, Helen,

speaker: COUNTESS
text: If you should tender your supposèd aid,

speaker: COUNTESS
text: He would receive it? He and his physicians

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Are of a mind: he that they cannot help him,

speaker: COUNTESS
text: They that they cannot help. How shall they credit

speaker: COUNTESS
text: A poor unlearnèd virgin, when the schools

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Emboweled of their doctrine have left off

speaker: COUNTESS
text: The danger to itself?

speaker: HELEN
text: There’s something in ’t

speaker: HELEN
text: More than my father’s skill, which was the great’st

speaker: HELEN
text: Of his profession, that his good receipt

speaker: HELEN
text: Shall for my legacy be sanctified

speaker: HELEN
text: By th’ luckiest stars in heaven; and would your Honor

speaker: HELEN
text: But give me leave to try success, I’d venture

speaker: HELEN
text: The well-lost life of mine on his Grace’s cure

speaker: HELEN
text: By such a day, an hour.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Dost thou believe ’t?

speaker: HELEN
text: Ay, madam, knowingly.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Why, Helen, thou shalt have my leave and love,

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Means and attendants, and my loving greetings

speaker: COUNTESS
text: To those of mine in court. I’ll stay at home

speaker: COUNTESS
text: And pray God’s blessing into thy attempt.

speaker: COUNTESS
text: Be gone tomorrow, and be sure of this:

speaker: COUNTESS
text: What I can help thee to thou shalt not miss.
